mnemonics.title: Cache Performance Formula
mnemonics.content: AMAT = Hit Time + (Miss Rate × Miss Penalty) - Think: 'Always Making A Trade-off' between speed and accuracy

mnemonics.title: Locality Types
mnemonics.content: Temporal = Time-based (same location again); Spatial = Space-based (neighbor locations). Remember: 'TEmporal = Time, SPAtial = Space'

mnemonics.title: Cache Write Policies
mnemonics.content: Write-Through: 'Write Twice' (cache + memory); Write-Back: 'Wait Until Back' (eviction time). Remember: 'Through = Done Right Away, Back = Later'

mnemonics.title: Cache Mapping Techniques
mnemonics.content: Direct: 'Fixed Address'; Set-Assoc: 'Grouped Flexibility'; Fully-Assoc: 'Anywhere Possible'. Remember: 'Direct = Direct, Set = Grouped, Fully = Free'

acronyms.usage: Performance metric combining hit time and miss penalty
Cache replacement algorithm prioritizing recent accesses
Cache replacement based on age of data
Cache replacement based on access frequency
Method for bypassing CPU in memory transfers
Hardware used in fully associative caches for fast tag lookup
Cache addressing method in virtual memory systems

visualTips.visualization: Draw address as three boxes: [TAG] [INDEX] [OFFSET]. Offset determines byte within line, Index selects cache set, Tag identifies the block. Example: 32-bit address, 64-byte line, 256 sets = 6 index bits, 6 offset bits, 20 tag bits
Start → Check Valid + Tag Match → Hit: Return Data / Miss: Fetch from Memory → Update Cache → Return Data. Draw this as a simple decision diamond and process boxes
Show cache line with fields: [Valid][Dirty][Tag][Data]. Dirty bit = 0 (clean, no write-back needed), Dirty bit = 1 (modified, must write back on eviction)
Draw pyramid: L1 (top, smallest, fastest), L2 (middle), L3 (bottom, largest, slowest). Show how data flows from L1 to main memory on miss, each level larger and slower

analogies.analogy: Main memory is a library (far away, slow). Cache is your desk (nearby, fast). You keep frequently used books at your desk. When you need a book, you first check your desk (cache hit); if not there, go to library (cache miss) and bring it back to desk.

Direct mapping = reserved parking spots (one per car, may have conflict). Fully associative = anywhere in lot (flexible but need to search all). Set-associative = parking zones with multiple spots (compromise).
Write-through = taking notes and immediately copying to shared file (safe but slow). Write-back = taking notes and only copying to shared file when you leave (fast but risky if crash).
